mechanism: Inhibits DNA methylation, leading to reactivation of tumor suppressor genes
additional_notes: Used in myelodysplastic syndromes and AML.

mechanism: Inhibits DNA methylation, induces apoptosis in cancer cells
additional_notes: Similar to Azacitidine, used for myelodysplastic syndromes.

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 to promote apoptosis in malignant cells
additional_notes: Approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia; under investigation for AML.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, leading to decreased proliferation and survival of malignancies
additional_notes: Used in myelofibrosis.

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and SRC family kinases, leading to reduced cell proliferation
additional_notes: Originally used for chronic myeloid leukemia.

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL kinase, blocking cell division in specific cancers
additional_notes: First-line treatment for CML.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor kinases involved in tumor cell proliferation and angiogenesis
additional_notes: Approved for renal cell carcinoma and gastrointestinal stromal tumors.

mechanism: Inhibits ribonucleotide reductase, reducing nucleotide synthesis and slowing cell division
additional_notes: Used commonly in sickle cell disease and some leukemias.

mechanism: Induces apoptosis and differentiation in acute promyelocytic leukemia cells
additional_notes: Effective for acute promyelocytic leukemia.

mechanism: Modulates immune system with anti-angiogenic and anti-inflammatory properties
additional_notes: Previously withdrawn from the market; now used in multiple myeloma.

mechanism: Enhances immune response and inhibits proliferation of tumor cells
additional_notes: Analog of Thalidomide, used in multiple myeloma.

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinase, VEGFR, and PDGFR, impacting tumor proliferation and angiogenesis
additional_notes: Primarily used for renal cell carcinoma and hepatocellular carcinoma.

mechanism: IL-6 receptor antagonist, reduces inflammation and immune activation
additional_notes: Used to treat rheumatoid arthritis; under investigation for various cancers.

mechanism: Inhibits DNA synthesis and promotes apoptosis in cancer cells
additional_notes: Used in pediatric and adult acute leukemia.

mechanism: Inhibits proteasome function, leading to accumulation of pro-apoptotic factors
additional_notes: Used in multiple myeloma.

mechanism: Enhances immune response and inhibits the growth of cancer cells
additional_notes: Similar to Thalidomide and Lenalidomide.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, leading to altered gene expression and apoptosis in cancer cells
additional_notes: FDA approved for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma.

mechanism: Restores function to mutated p53 protein, promoting apoptosis in cancer cells
additional_notes: Example includes PRIMA-1.

mechanism: Decreases hepatic glucose production and may affect cancer metabolism
additional_notes: Commonly used for type 2 diabetes.

mechanism: Inhibits microtubule formation, disrupting cellular mitosis
additional_notes: Used for various hematological malignancies.
